title: Causal Organizational Control
objective: Determine whether coherent-generative organizational regimes can be caused, stabilized, destabilized, or transitioned through controlled intervention.

outputs.attractor_analysis.csv: 31 regime attractor entries with self-transition and escape probabilities
outputs.metastability_metrics.csv: 10 per-domain metastability entropy and hysteresis scores
outputs.minimal_conditions.csv: 2372 CG-entry interventions (transitions to constrained generativity from non-CG)
outputs.causal_transfer.csv: 15 cross-family causality transfer entries across 3 geometry clusters

key_findings.cg_inducibility: CG is causally inducible in 6/10 domains but inaccessible in 4 (cellular_automata, graph_diffusion, lotka_volterra, replicator).
key_findings.best_triggers: Most effective interventions: nonlinear_oscillator-RTP (48.2% CG entry), gray_scott-CSR (35.2%), kuramoto-SRD (31.3%).
key_findings.attractor_structure: CG is NOT an attractor (self-transition=0.36, escape=0.64). Chaotic_fertility is strongest attractor (self=0.57).
key_findings.destruction: RBS perturbation is the single most destructive intervention across 5/6 CG-capable domains.
key_findings.transfer_failure: Causal effects do NOT transfer across geometry clusters. Cluster 2 has highest within-cluster variance (ratio=2.95-4.40).
key_findings.null_survival: RTP and ADI survive random-descriptor null (z=4.07, -2.63). CSR, RBS, SRD are indistinguishable from random perturbations.
key_findings.hysteresis: Global hysteresis asymmetry ranges from -2.53 (branching) to 0.18 (gray_scott), indicating mostly reversible transitions.
key_findings.primary_conclusion: Coherent-generative regimes can be causally induced in some domains but are not stable attractors. Causality is family-specific, not universal.

files.phaseH1H2_interventions.py: Target regime identification + matched-pair perturbation simulation
files.phaseH3H4H5_maps_attractors.py: Transition probability maps, attractor analysis, minimal conditions search
files.phaseH6H7H8_destruction_transfer_nulls.p: Destruction tests, cross-family causality transfer, null program
